feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Why men die younger than women — and the tough guy paradox that drives doctors nuts
entry.summary: Women live longer than men, all over the world. In the US, men live around 76 years, while women live til 81.

entry.title: Strange Side Effect Found Among Ozempic Users—and It Has to Do With Smell and Taste
entry.summary: New research has identified a potential, if small, added risk of smell and taste disturbances linked to GLP-1 use.

entry.title: Retraction of high-profile paper on cancer drug timing could hand ammo to China hawks
entry.summary: Nature Medicine retracts China lung cancer immunotherapy timing paper, citing data integrity concerns — and the fallout could fuel US anti-China sentiment in biopharma.

entry.title: Students warned of possible tuberculosis at metro Atlanta high school
entry.summary: The Fulton County Board of Health sent a letter to parents of a county high school to inform them that students and staff might have been exposed to someone diagnosed with tuberculosis.

entry.title: 30 going on 21: Bryan Johnson’s girlfriend reveals extreme biohacking that made her skin age backwards
entry.summary: Kate Tolo, the girlfriend of biohacker Bryan Johnson, has revealed her skin is nine years younger than she is — but, she added, that doesn’t mean she necessarily looks younger.

entry.title: BIO 2026: How a gut feeling transformed cancer treatment forever
entry.summary: Dr. | Dr. Israel Lowy remembers the days when he and his oncology immunotherapy colleagues were assigned tables in the distant east wing of the American Society of Clinical Oncology conference in Chicago. They would watch aficionados walk by, scoffing at thes…

entry.title: Wiggling Your Pinky Helps Prevent Alzheimer's, TikTok Says. Here's The Truth.
entry.summary: What if protecting your brain from dementia was as simple as wiggling your little fingers a few seconds each day? That's the promise behind "pinky time", a viral TikTok trend that claims a simple finger exercise can lower your risk of developing Alzheimer's.

entry.title: North Carolina has highest venomous snakebite rate in US, report shows
entry.summary: A report says the Tar Heel State ranks above every other state.

entry.title: Heavy Weights or Light Weights for Heart Health? A Doctor Explains
entry.summary: What are the best weights to use when strength training for heart health? Doctor explains why lifting lighter weights with more repetitions is better.

entry.title: Popular pain supplement linked to faster Alzheimer’s decline — and death
entry.summary: While many turn to a popular supplement to ease joint pain, new research suggests they could actually speed up cognitive decline.

entry.title: I lost my hearing at 32. Recognizing it as an emergency could have helped.
entry.summary: The loss in one ear is permanent, and I have ongoing tinnitus as a side effect of a widely misdiagnosed condition.

entry.title: Latest COVID vaccine may have unexpected health benefit, study suggests
entry.summary: New research links the 2024-2025 COVID-19 vaccine to fewer serious cardiovascular events among U.S. veterans, especially those over age 75.

entry.title: These Beloved Snacks Are Linked To An Increased Dementia Risk. Here's What Doctors Want You To Know.
entry.summary: "You can't necessarily salad your way out" of this snacking habit, one doctor noted.

entry.title: Exclusive | New Yorkers are bonding over loss — and cardio — in ‘Grief, Sweat, & Tears’ workout classes
entry.summary: It’s an exclusive club no one wants to be a member of, as several people who lost a loved one gathered for a Grief, Sweat, & Tears double-header barre and spin class.

entry.title: After a brain injury, he found purpose fixing bikes no one else would bother with
entry.summary: Rob, 54, forgot how to do his job after a car hit him in a crosswalk, leaving him with cognitive and physical impairment. But he still remembered how to fix bicycles.

entry.title: Alzheimer’s Risk May Be Determined by One Daily Habit Combined With Genes
entry.summary: The study revealed that certain sleep disturbances are linked to faster loss of gray matter and reduced brain volume.

entry.title: How familiar are you with your testicles? A urologist explains what’s normal and what’s not
entry.summary: Getting to know your body through regular testicular self-exams can help you spot changes early. A urologist breaks down what to look for in periodic checks.

entry.title: Benefits of finerenone treatment across chronic kidney disease aetiologies
entry.summary: Nature Reviews Nephrology - Benefits of finerenone treatment across chronic kidney disease aetiologies

entry.title: First Ebola Case Outside Africa in 2026 Outbreak Confirmed
entry.summary: France on Wednesday announced the first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor who had flown back from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is fighting a major outbreak.

entry.title: The Subtle Physical Clue That Could Indicate Your Dog Has Dementia
entry.summary: Dementia affects dogs as well as people, and although it goes by a different name, it's no less devastating for owners of beloved pets.

entry.title: Man survives infection by extremely rare, dangerous bacteria while fishing off Dauphin Island
entry.summary: DAUPHIN ISLAND, Ala. (WKRG) — A Mobile man is recovering after becoming infected by an extremely rare bacteria he contracted from the warm Gulf waters. Joel Kotowske is finally back home after contracting Sphingomonas Paucimobilis while fishing off Dauphin Is…

entry.title: How to recognize signs of a stroke and act as soon as possible
entry.summary: Neurologists want patients to understand that many stroke cases may be prevented. If a stroke occurs, treatment is possible.

entry.title: Antibiotic cocktail made by soil bacteria can kill superbugs
entry.summary: Four antibiotic compounds produced in Streptomyces bacteria attack multiple parts of an essential metabolic pathway.

entry.title: 5 Carbs People with Heart Disease Should Be Eating, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Dietitians say these heart-healthy carbs are rich in fiber and nutrients that support better cholesterol, blood pressure and overall health.

entry.title: Measles case confirmed in person who traveled through Hollywood Burbank Airport
entry.summary: Los Angeles County health officials are investigating a confirmed case of measles in a person who was infectious while traveling through Hollywood Burbank Airport and at least one other location. This is the seventh case of measles reported by L.A. County Pub…

entry.title: CDC concludes hantavirus response as outbreak eases
entry.summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said on Wednesday it ​ended responses to the hantavirus outbreak linked to a cruise ship, two months after the virus killed three people.

entry.title: ‘Frankenstein rabbits’ with tentacles coming from head reemerge as warning issued
entry.summary: Scary stuff...

entry.title: Obesity cases rising fastest in young adults
entry.summary: Experts say the cost of living, pandemic and boom in unhealthy food are behind the rise in cases.

entry.title: 10 early dementia warning signs everyone should know, according to the CDC
entry.summary: Neural Effects consulted the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's (CDC) list of warning signs for dementia and Alzheimer's disease for a list of 10 potential early signs of dementia.

entry.title: An ECG biomarker for sudden cardiac death discovered with deep learning
entry.summary: A deep-learning model trained on electrocardiogram (ECG) waveforms identifies an easily visible biomarker that predicts sudden cardiac death more accurately than the current clinical state of the art.

entry.title: For decades, scientists repeated as established fact that bacteria in the human body outnumber human cells ten to one — a 2016 study by the Weizmann Institute of Science reviewed the original source and found the actual ratio is closer to 1:1, making the figu
entry.summary: For a long time, one number did a great deal of work in popular accounts of human biology. Ten to one: for every cell that was genetically yours, ten bacteria were along for the ride, making you, in some loosely held sense, more microbial than human. The figu…

entry.title: Beware ‘the narrowing’ — doctor warns it can steal your strength as you age
entry.summary: If you’re an older adult, you’ve probably fallen into the trap one doctor calls “the narrowing.”

entry.title: US ends hantavirus outbreak response with no answers on draconian quarantines
entry.summary: We still don't know why RFK Jr. overruled CDC expert to order strict quarantines.

entry.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
entry.summary: The patient is in isolation, following strict biosafety protocols to prevent risk of spread in France.

entry.title: Uneven cerebellum aging may partly explain why some older adults stay mentally sharp
entry.summary: Scientists may have discovered a new role for the cerebellum, the part of the brain that sits at the base of the skull. A new paper published in the journal Nature Neuroscience reports that different parts of the cerebellum change at different rates with age,…

entry.title: What was in the COVID-19 vaccine study CDC withheld?
entry.summary: Why did CDC not publish taxpayer-funded COVID vaccine study? What did the COVID study find? Did CDC withhold vaccine study for political reasons?

entry.title: Micro-workouts: how to get fitter and healthier in 5 minutes
entry.summary: Taking ‘movement breaks’ at work reduces the risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes and cancer, a study has found. They’re not the only quick-fix wellbeing boosters backed by science

entry.title: France reports first Ebola patient as cases in Africa surge above 1,000
entry.summary: France said a doctor back from a humanitarian mission was infected and in stable condition, while experts said the outbreak in Africa could be the worst ever recorded.

entry.title: Three Las Vegas area ZIP codes report West Nile virus-positive mosquitoes
entry.summary: Mosquitoes collected in the 89121 ZIP code in southeast Las Vegas first tested positive as part of the Health District’s ongoing surveillance program. The agency later announced ...

entry.title: U.S. provides experimental Ebola treatment for outbreak in Congo, bringing trials closer
entry.summary: The U.S. had said the antibody drug would be made available ​only to Americans deemed to be at high risk.

entry.title: Scientists observe water switching between 2 different molecular structures, boosting decades-old theory
entry.summary: For decades, scientists suspected water secretly behaves like two different liquids. A new AI-powered study has finally caught it happening at the molecular level.

entry.title: Why you should be brushing your teeth with your left hand to prevent dementia
entry.summary: The secret to a better brain workout might already be sitting in your bathroom.

entry.title: Here’s why you should be more worried about tetanus
entry.summary: The disease can cause muscle spasms strong enough to fracture bones. It’s fully preventable, but vaccination rates are slipping.

entry.title: 'Rat infestation' at Austin hospital forces kitchen closure, more cleaning
entry.summary: The hospital has closed its kitchen while treating the problem found during new construction project.

entry.title: North Carolina has highest venomous snakebite rate in U.S., new report shows
entry.summary: North Carolina has the highest estimated rate of venomous snakebites per capita in the United States, according to a 2026 national analysis.

entry.title: I Was Fine to Give the Guy I Was Hooking Up With an Assist the First Few Times. But Three Strikes and You’re Out.
entry.summary: I'm not this selfless.

entry.title: People With Eating Disorders Are Misusing Ozempic
entry.summary: New research finds that rates of GLP-1 use and misuse are shockingly high among people with a history of eating disorders.

entry.title: Yale study finds the secret to aging well over 65
entry.summary: Over 60 million adults are now over the age of 65 in the US, representing about 18% of the population. And they’re living longer too — the death rate for older adults is the lowest since 1999…

entry.title: Chicago health officials warn of possible measles exposure at O’Hare airport
entry.summary: Officials from the Chicago Department of Public Health (CDPH) say they are investigating a confirmed case of measles in an international traveler who arrived in Chicago on June 17.

entry.title: Nature retracts provocative PD-1 study that tied lung cancer survival to treatment timing
entry.summary: A Nature Medicine paper landed on Feb. | After a four-month investigation, Nature Medicine has decided to pull a viral study that linked early-day PD-1/chemo treatment to a massive survival benefit versus late-day infusion in non-small cell lung cancer.
